questions.question: What are the main differences between cloud bursting and cloud federation?
expectedPoints: Cloud bursting is temporary use of external resources for capacity overflow
Cloud federation involves formal agreements for resource sharing between providers
Bursting is primarily consumer-focused, federation is provider-focused
Federation implies ongoing relationships, bursting is event-driven

questions.question: Explain the broker-based architecture for inter-cloud resource management, including its advantages and disadvantages.
expectedPoints: Central intermediary between consumers and providers
Handles service discovery, SLA negotiation, resource allocation
Advantages: Simplified management, single point of control
Disadvantages: Single point of failure, potential bottleneck
Suitable for enterprises with complex multi-cloud needs
Examples: Cloud management platforms, brokerage services
